name: Korean
register: Reverent contemporary Korean Bible register keyed to 개역개정 (the Korean Revised Version / Gaeyeok Gaejeong) — the most-trusted and most-read Protestant text in Korean churches — warmed toward the readability of 새번역 (New Korean Revised Standard). God is always addressed with subject-honorific verb morphology (~시-, 계시다, 하시다) and the literary vocative 아버지여 / warm 아버지, so the reader hears a beloved child speaking to their own father in the cadence of family prayer and the Lord's Prayer.
quote_convention: Horizontal double quotation marks " " (큰따옴표) for direct speech, single ' ' (작은따옴표) for quotation-within-quotation; traditional corner brackets 「」 / 『』 acceptable in editions following the older typographic tradition.
poetry_convention: Poetry (Psalms, Prophets, canticles) set as broken poetic lines rather than running prose; Hebrew parallel couplets placed one line each, the second (parallel) line indented under the first, following the 새번역 poetic layout.

glossary.gloss: our Father who is in heaven
glossary.note: Divine name. Exact wording of the Lord's Prayer opening in 개역개정 (하늘에 계신 우리 아버지여) — instantly recognized as address to the Father, never to 하나님.
Primary filial divine name. Plain 아버지 (respectful 'father', not the generic deity word); the possessive 우리 makes it belonging, warm. NEVER 하나님 / 여호와.
Definite filial name; Korean has no article, so bare 아버지 in divine context reads as 'the Father'. Carries honorific verb agreement to mark deity.
The standing form of address in Korean hymns and congregational prayer; keeps the filial 아버지 stem while marking transcendence with 하늘.
Exactly the 개역개정 rendering (Mark 14:36; Rom 8:15; Gal 4:6). 아빠 is the intimate child's 'daddy' (Aramaic Abba retained in sense), paired with reverent 아버지 — the tenderness the vision requires.
REVERSION TOKEN — traditional Protestant 개역개정 word for God-as-God (Catholic tradition uses 하느님). Non-filial contrast term; never a substitute for 아버지.
REVERSION TOKEN — the covenant name as vocalized in 개역개정. Non-filial contrast term. Generic 'Lord' for Jesus = 주 / 주님, distinct from 여호와.
Universal Korean Bible term (聖靈); fixed and unambiguous.
Relational genitive with 영 ('spirit'), NOT the by-identity title 성령 — marks the Spirit as belonging to the Father, per the preview's relational rendering.
Standard 개역개정 Christological title 인자(人子). NB machine-check: homograph of 인자(仁慈) 'loving-kindness' below — disambiguate by hanja/context, not Hangul.
Bare 아들 ('son') for Jesus as the Son; pairs naturally with the filial 아버지 to render the Father–Son relation.
Traditional 개역개정 title (retains reversion-token 하나님 because English keeps 'God'). Proposed — native reviewer to confirm vs the filial 아버지의 아들 ('the Father's Son').
The beloved 개역개정 word at John 3:16 (獨生子); deeply familiar to Korean believers. Alt for readability register: 외아들 ('only son').
'Kingdom of God' rendered filially: traditional 하나님의 나라 → 아버지의 나라, keeping 나라 ('kingdom/realm').
Text form 예수 (예수님 with honorific 님 in address/reference); universal Korean Bible spelling.
Standard transliteration in all Korean Bibles.
Transliterated title (older 개역: 메시야). Used where the text names the awaited Anointed One.
Traditional 개역개정 term 언약 (over legal-flavored 계약); the biblical word for God's binding promise.
Renders Hebrew hesed as loyal, unwavering love. Proposed — reviewer to confirm vs the traditional 인자(하심) 'loving-kindness' (仁慈; note homograph with 인자 'Son of Man').
Traditional 개역개정 word 긍휼 for racham / splanchnizomai — gut-level tender mercy; God 'being moved with 긍휼.' Alt narrative verb: 불쌍히 여기시다.
Standard 개역개정 term 의 (義), e.g. 하나님의 의 'the righteousness of God.'
The 개역개정 word at John 3:16 (永生); fixed and beloved. Alt: 영원한 생명.

glossary.gloss: to be born again / born anew
glossary.note: The 개역개정 wording at John 3 (거듭나지 아니하면… 'unless one is born again'). Greek anōthen also 'from above' (위로부터 나다), but 거듭나다 is the established, recognizable form.
